metadata.title: VoidAd — Your Digital Neutral Zone
metadata.description: DNS-level network security, ad blocking, and privacy protection for every device on your network.

hero.titleLine3: Your Privacy Remains. Securing Your Digital Space.
hero.title: Reclaim Your Internet.
hero.subtitle: VoidAd filters threats at the network level — every device on your router is protected automatically.

settings.title: Account & Profile Settings
settings.guestPrompt: Sign in to manage your account, privacy, and notification preferences.
settings.saveFailed: Could not save changes. Try again.
settings.avatarFailed: Could not upload picture. Try again.

dashboard.title: Features & Control
dashboard.guestPrompt: Sign in to sync protection settings and VoidPoints across devices.
dashboard.donateHint: Every blocked ad earns 1 VoidPoint. Donate your points to carbon offset charities.
dashboard.noActivity: No activity yet — protection is active and waiting.
dashboard.statsNote: Updated live from your home Wi‑Fi DNS
dashboard.maxProtectionDesc: Enables all VoidAd layers at once. This is the strongest setup for Mac/PC browsers and your home network.
dashboard.maxStep1: Layer 1: DNS on router/Mac (450k+ domains + rotator blocking)
dashboard.maxStep2: Layer 2: Chrome extension (popups, streaming, YouTube skip)
dashboard.maxStep3: Chrome: Secure DNS OFF + block pop-ups
dashboard.maxLimitNote: Netflix & YouTube TV apps still need Premium/ad-free plans — no technology can block same-server video ads on smart TVs.

familyFilters.title: Family & Content Filters
familyFilters.subtitle: Real DNS blocking for TikTok, adult content, gambling, and your custom keywords. Changes sync to your home network within ~60 seconds.
familyFilters.blockTiktokHint: Stops tiktok.com, app CDNs, and ByteDance hosts
familyFilters.blockSocialHint: Instagram, Facebook, X, Snapchat, Reddit, Discord, Twitch
familyFilters.blockAdultHint: Major adult sites + suspicious domain labels
familyFilters.blockGamblingHint: Casino rotators, betting sites, hitnspin-style domains
familyFilters.safeSearchHint: Extra adult-domain blocking for child devices
familyFilters.keywordsHint: Blocks domains whose name contains your words (min 3 characters). Example: casino, porn, bet
familyFilters.dnsNote: DNS blocks apps and browsers on your home Wi‑Fi when devices use VoidAd DNS (192.168.0.119). For Chrome/Edge bypass, keep the VoidAd extension enabled.
familyFilters.childActiveNote: Child profile active — TikTok, social, adult, and gambling filters are enforced.

default.title: Standard
default.desc: Ads, trackers, and phishing only. You choose extra filters.

child.title: Child
child.desc: TikTok off, social limited, adult & gambling blocked, strict ads.

strict.title: Strict
strict.desc: Child profile + geo-block and focus mode for maximum control.

voidpoints.title: Make a Difference with Your VoidPoints
voidpoints.noRecentDonations: No donations yet — your impact starts with your first contribution.
voidpoints.signInForActivity: Sign in to see your donation history.
voidpoints.levelLockedAria: Level {level} — locked until you reach this stage

about.title: About VoidAd
about.missionText: VoidAd is an integrated digital security platform built on DNS-level filtering. We protect privacy, block malware and phishing, and turn saved bandwidth into VoidPoints for charity.
about.privacy: Privacy First — zero-log policy, no ad revenue model.
about.security: Network-Wide Security — every connected device protected.
about.social: Social Impact — convert savings into real-world good.
about.hardware: VoidAd-Box — future hardware solution for smart homes (coming soon).

auth.signupSubtitle: Start your 24h Trial — one account per device & network
auth.passwordHint: At least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, a number, and a symbol (e.g. !@#$).
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.passwordNeedsUpper: Include at least one uppercase letter (A–Z).
auth.passwordNeedsLower: Include at least one lowercase letter (a–z).
auth.confirmEmail: Check your email and click the confirmation link to activate your account.
auth.confirmEmailSent: Confirmation email sent. Check your inbox.
auth.trialNote: Trial plan: 24 hours. One Trial/Free account per device and home network.
auth.devicePolicy: To prevent abuse, each device and router (network) can only register one Trial or Free account. Paid plans (Pro/Ultimate) are not limited.
auth.emailNotConfirmed: Please confirm your email before signing in.
auth.deviceAlreadyRegistered: This device or network already has a Trial/Free account. Sign in with your existing account or upgrade to Pro.
auth.deviceAlreadyRegisteredTitle: This device or network already has a Trial/Free account.
auth.deviceAlreadyRegisteredSignIn: Sign in with your existing account
auth.deviceMismatch: This account is locked to another device or network. Trial/Free plans allow one device and router only.
auth.serverNotReady: Server auth is not ready. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL on Vercel (or fix anon key) and redeploy.
auth.databaseNotReady: Database not ready. Run supabase/migrations/002 and 003 in SQL Editor.
auth.notConfigured: Authentication is not configured on this server.
auth.unexpectedError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

extension.installDesc: DNS (Layer 1) blocks 450k+ ad networks on your Wi‑Fi. YouTube, streaming sites, and popups need the browser extension (Layer 2) — install once, protection runs automatically.
extension.featureYoutube: Skip YouTube video ads and hide sidebar / in-feed ads
extension.featureStreaming: Clean first-party ads on streaming sites (faselhd, shahid4u, etc.)
extension.featurePopups: Block pop-up windows and casino redirect tabs
extension.featureDnsSync: Report new ad domains to your DNS proxy for whole-network blocking
extension.featureVoidpoints: Every block earns VoidPoints — shown live on your dashboard
extension.featureBrowser: Works in Chrome and Edge (Chromium) — no extra config
extension.step1: Click Download Extension above and unzip the folder on your computer
extension.step2: Open Chrome → paste chrome://extensions in the address bar (use Copy button above)
extension.step3: Enable Developer mode (top right) → Load unpacked → select the unzipped folder (do NOT open popup.html directly)
extension.step4: Sign in to voidad.com — the extension syncs your account automatically
extension.step5: Chrome Settings → Privacy → Security → turn OFF “Use secure DNS”
extension.step6: Refresh YouTube or any streaming site — ads should skip or disappear
extension.copiedChrome: Copied! Paste in Chrome address bar
extension.copyPrompt: Copy this URL and paste in Chrome:
extension.copied: Path copied! Open chrome://extensions and load unpacked.
extension.activeDesc: Dual-layer protection is on: DNS blocks network ads; extension cleans YouTube, streaming UI, and popups. Re-download below if you need to reinstall on another PC.
extension.liveBlocked: {count, plural, =0 {No ads blocked yet in browser} one {# ad blocked in browser} other {# ads blocked in browser}}
extension.protectionOff: Protection is off in the extension
extension.pinHint: Pin VoidAd to the toolbar to see the live blocked count badge.

networkDns.title: Your VoidAd DNS
networkDns.subtitleShort: Copy the DNS address below → paste it on your phone or computer. All devices on your Wi‑Fi will block ads automatically.
networkDns.usingDefaultDns: Using default DNS. Link your account after registering Wi‑Fi.
networkDns.deviceStepsTitle: How to set DNS on your devices
networkDns.iphoneStep1: Settings → Wi‑Fi → (i) next to your network
networkDns.iphoneStep3: Turn off iCloud Private Relay if enabled (Settings → Apple ID → iCloud)
networkDns.androidStep1: Settings → Network → Wi‑Fi → hold your network → Modify
networkDns.windowsStep1: Settings → Network & Internet → Wi‑Fi → your network → Edit
networkDns.windowsStep2: Set DNS to Manual → Preferred DNS: {primary}
networkDns.subtitle: Layer 1 (DNS): copy one address to your router — blocks 450k+ ad domains on every device. Layer 2 (extension): required for streaming sites with first-party ads.
networkDns.blackHoleNote: VoidAd DNS uses the OISD Big list (~450,000 ad & tracker domains). Blocked domains resolve to nothing — like a black hole. Works on phones, TVs, laptops, and tablets automatically.
networkDns.youtubeLimit: YouTube in-video ads come from youtube.com / googlevideo.com — DNS cannot block them without breaking playback. On PC: VoidAd extension skips ads. On TV app: YouTube Premium required.
networkDns.tvNetflixLimit: Smart TV / Netflix / YouTube apps: in-video ads are first-party (same servers as the video). VoidAd DNS on your router blocks trackers and third-party ad networks on the TV, but cannot remove ads inside the Netflix or YouTube TV apps. Netflix: use the ad-free plan. YouTube on TV: YouTube Premium, or watch via browser on a laptop with the extension.
networkDns.flashWarning: For streaming sites (faselhd, shahid4u): use DNS + VoidAd Chrome extension together. DNS alone cannot block popups and in-page ads.
networkDns.chromeDohSteps: Chrome → Settings → Privacy and security → Security → turn OFF “Use secure DNS”. Otherwise Chrome bypasses your router DNS and ads still load.
networkDns.dnsOnlyTitle: Use VoidAd DNS only — no backup
networkDns.dnsOnlySteps: Use ONLY {primary} as DNS. Remove 1.1.1.1 and 8.8.8.8 completely — do NOT add them as search domains (causes ads to bypass blocking).
networkDns.macDnsFix: Mac fix: System Settings → Network → Wi‑Fi → Details → DNS → delete 1.1.1.1 if present → keep only 127.0.0.1 → Apply.
networkDns.verifyExpected: Must return 0.0.0.0 (not a real IP). If you see numbers like 216.x.x.x, restart DNS: cd dev/dns-proxy && ./run.sh
networkDns.restartDns: Restart DNS server after updates: cd dev/dns-proxy && ./run.sh
networkDns.firstPartyLimit: Some sites (e.g. streaming sites) show ads from their own domain — DNS cannot block those. Third-party ad networks (popups, casino, trackers) are blocked.
networkDns.whereToPut: You do NOT type DNS here. Copy Primary DNS → paste it in your router admin page (or Mac Network settings).
networkDns.copyToRouter: Paste this in router DNS settings
networkDns.routerStep1: Open router admin: usually http://192.168.0.1 or http://192.168.1.1
networkDns.routerStep2: Find DNS settings → set Primary DNS to {primary}
networkDns.routerStep3: Click “Register this Wi‑Fi” below while on your home network
networkDns.routerStep4: Save router → all phones, TVs, laptops are protected
networkDns.lanProxyDesc: Run VoidAd on your Mac as the network DNS server. Phones, TVs, and laptops on the same Wi‑Fi will send queries to your Mac. Device IP appears in the proxy log (phone vs TV vs laptop).
networkDns.lanProxyOffline: Proxy not detected on this Mac
networkDns.lanProxyCommand: Start LAN mode on this Mac
networkDns.lanProxyLiveStats: Live on this Mac: {blocked} blocked · {forwarded} forwarded — open http://127.0.0.1:8053 for device IPs
networkDns.lanProxyDashboardHint: Open this dashboard on the same Mac while ./run.sh is running to see live stats.
networkDns.lanStep1: On your Mac: cd dev/dns-proxy && VOIDAD_LAN_MODE=true ./run.sh (keep Mac awake)
networkDns.lanStep2: Router admin (e.g. 192.168.0.1) → set Primary DNS to {primary}
networkDns.lanStep3: Set Secondary DNS to {fallback} so the home keeps internet if the Mac sleeps
networkDns.lanStep4: Click Register this Wi‑Fi below — check proxy logs for each device IP
networkDns.macStep1: System Settings → Network → Wi‑Fi → Details → DNS
networkDns.macStep3: Click OK → Apply, then Register this Wi‑Fi below
networkDns.networkRegistered: Home network registered. DNS filtering is now linked to your account.
networkDns.noNetworkYet: After setting router DNS, click Register this Wi‑Fi.
networkDns.signInRequired: Sign in to get your personal DNS settings.
networkDns.migrationRequired: Database not ready — run migrations 006 and 007 in Supabase SQL Editor.

errors.INVALID_IP: Could not detect your network IP.
errors.REGISTER_FAILED: Registration failed. Run migrations 006–008 in Supabase.
